title: geranium pusillum var. humile
intro: Geranium pusillum var. humile, also known as the dwarf or low-growing geranium, is a low-growing perennial plant species native to North America, characterized by its small, pink or white flowers and delicate, hairy leaves. This plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce inflammation, and fight off infections. The health benefits of Geranium pusillum var. humile include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce pain and swelling, and support the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for various health conditions. Geranium pusillum var. humile can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used topically to apply its medicinal properties directly to the skin.
